Landing.hero_body: Generate da'wah content kits from the up-to-date Indonesian media landscape — Friday khutbah, kajian outlines, video scripts, flyers, and local action plans — every week, grounded in verified kitab daleel.
Landing.hero_insights_card_body: See this week's Indonesian media briefing — daleel-grounded, free, no signup needed.
Landing.hero_no_login_required: No login required — start reading right away
Landing.hero_login_purpose: Sign up only if you want briefings personalized to your community. Beta quota is limited, so we review each sign-up to make sure personalized features go to people who actually need them. All public features stay open without login.
Landing.hero_request_access: Contact the team for beta access
Landing.signup_section_title: What changes if you sign up?
Landing.signup_section_subtitle: The public briefings at /insights already cover most da'wah needs. A registered account adds a layer of personalization tailored to your community — plus manual team review to maintain quality.
Landing.signup_benefit_personalized_body: Not generic templates — da'wah angles tailored to your community profile: jamaah size, segment focus, mazhab, regional language style, and the local issues that matter right now.
Landing.signup_benefit_saved_body: Bookmark daleel, kajian, and briefings to revisit later. Your reading history is private to you, not shared with anyone else.
Landing.signup_benefit_reviewed_body: Beta quota is limited, so we review each sign-up to make sure personalized features land with people who actually need them. Once approved, we also do periodic manual reviews to maintain quality — catching incorrect daleel, awkward framing, or content that doesn't fit ahlu sunnah wal jama'ah manhaj.
Landing.signup_process_subtitle: 4 simple steps — we keep this ecosystem high quality by manually verifying every account.
Landing.signup_step_1_body: You fill in your profile: role (da'i, ustadzah, content creator, parent, community organizer), jamaah size, da'wah focus, and relevant local context.
Landing.signup_step_2_body: Our team checks profile completeness and alignment with the platform's mission (rahma-driven da'wah, ahlu sunnah wal jama'ah, mazhab-neutral).
Landing.signup_step_3_body: For da'i / community-organizer accounts we do additional verification — sometimes via institution reference, sometimes via a brief conversation. Goal: ensure the account isn't for divisive content or anything contradicting ijma'.
Landing.signup_step_4_body: Once approved, you can generate custom briefings on specific themes, archive kajian, and receive personalized briefings via email.
Landing.signup_pause_title: Signups paused during the development phase
Landing.signup_pause_body: We're still building features. For now, we are not approving new signups — so we can focus on getting the system right first. Please use the public Insights instead (free, no login, published every week) — they cover full Friday khutbah, kajian outlines, content scripts, social-action plans, and verified daleel.
Landing.signup_closing_pointer: Right now your best path is the weekly public briefings. Signups will reopen after the development phase wraps up.
Landing.signup_cost_title: Registered users can generate content — and that can get expensive
Landing.signup_cost_body: Every brief you create makes a paid AI model call (Claude / Gemini Pro). The free public weekly briefings are subsidized by us and we control the cadence. But if every registered user can generate on demand, costs could grow exponentially — and our budget is very limited. We're still deciding whether to keep it fully free, add quotas (daily/monthly limits), or pass some cost to users.
Landing.signup_cost_apology: We're sorry in advance if we have to charge later — not for profit, just for sustainability. If you have a better idea (sponsorship, community-funded model, etc.) we'd love to hear it.
Landing.signed_in_final_cta_title: What will you address this week?
Landing.signed_in_final_cta_body: Pick a trending topic or write your own — we'll draft a brief grounded in real Qur'anic daleel.
Landing.pending_account_note: Your account is still pending review. You can explore public insights while you wait.
Landing.features_title: Four layers of the da'wah pipeline
Landing.features_subtitle: From raw media signals, through thematic analysis, to a weekly briefing ready for the mimbar or your feed.
Landing.feature_monitor_body: Mainstream Indonesian news (Detik, Kompas, Republika, Tempo, etc.) scanned every 2 hours, plus weekly X and verified YouTube channels spanning 9 da'wah categories. You no longer have to open ten apps every morning to catch what's trending.
Landing.feature_analyze_body: Every story is classified into 9 da'wah categories (akhlaq, aqidah, family, youth, social-justice, etc.) by Gemini Flash-Lite, sentiment captured via an event-valence model, and main themes auto-clustered every Thursday morning.
Landing.feature_briefing_body: 5 content kits ship every Thursday 05:00 WIB — one overall view plus four segments (spiritual, family, youth, justice). Each includes a khutbah, kajian outline, home + video scripts, a campus pack, a tiered action plan, and share-ready flyers.
Landing.feature_advise_body: Every daleel in the briefings is pulled FROM our Qdrant corpus (Qur'an Kemenag, Sahih Bukhari, Sahih Muslim, Riyad as-Salihin) — never invented by an LLM. Shown with Arabic + translation + page number, traceable to source.
Landing.briefing_showcase_title: A full week's content kit, ready to adapt — no signup, no queue.
Landing.briefing_showcase_body: Every Thursday at 05:00 WIB we publish a fresh content kit — ready a day before Friday khutbah. Khutbah, kajian, home scripts, video scripts, a Mahasiswa campus pack (poster + article + Q&A), age-segmented action plan, and 6 share-ready flyers including a timely sunnah invitation and one recitable du'a — all grounded in this week's real media coverage. No waiting for inspiration, no building from scratch. Just pick the segment that fits and adapt.
Landing.briefing_showcase_bullet_cadence: 5 new briefings every Thursday 05:00 WIB — a day before Friday khutbah
Landing.briefing_showcase_bullet_segments: 1 overall view + 4 per-topic (Spiritual, Family, Youth, Justice)
Landing.briefing_showcase_bullet_daleel: 8-10 daleel per briefing — retrieved from verified kitab (Qur'an, Bukhari, Muslim, Riyad), never LLM-hallucinated
Landing.briefing_showcase_bullet_discussion: A discussion room on every briefing — the community can weigh in and trade perspectives
Landing.briefing_showcase_bullet_free: Free & public — usable without login, shareable directly
Landing.briefing_showcase_kit_title: A 6,900-9,400-word content kit, ready to adapt to your context
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_khutbah_body: 2,300-3,200 words from mukadimah to closing du'a in Arabic transliteration. Just tailor to your community.
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_kajian_body: 45-minute format with 3 talking points, real news examples, and anticipated Q&A.
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_home_body: 3-4 scenarios for parents with kids (SD/SMP/SMA) — opening questions + anticipated responses.
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_content_body: 60-90 second ready-to-camera scripts for TikTok/IG Reels/Shorts: hook → body → CTA.
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_genz_body: Bulletin-board poster with provocative question + QR code → a magazine-style article page + realistic Q&A. Built for university noticeboards, also shareable digitally.
Landing.briefing_showcase_item_action_body: 4 concrete actions for kids / teens / adults / elderly — small effort, under IDR 500K per action.
Landing.why_llm_title: ChatGPT, Claude, DeepSeek already exist — why still need Dakwah-Lens?
Landing.why_llm_subtitle: For general queries, mainstream LLMs are more than enough. But for preparing da'wah content, four structural limits make general LLMs the wrong tool to lean on.
Landing.why_llm_row1_llm: Daleel can be invented. LLMs regularly output verses or hadith that DO NOT EXIST, or with wrong surah/hadith numbers — the single biggest credibility risk for a da'i.
Landing.why_llm_row1_us: Every daleel is RETRIEVED from a verified kitab corpus — Qur'an, Bukhari, Muslim, Riyad as-Salihin. The LLM is forbidden from inventing; if it's not in the corpus, it isn't cited.
Landing.why_llm_row2_llm: Can search the web, but lookups are ad-hoc and generic — you phrase the query, the LLM Googles, you parse the results. No memory of what mattered to Indonesian Muslims yesterday.
Landing.why_llm_row2_us: Ingests Indonesian RSS every 2 hours plus social weekly (X, YouTube, TikTok, Instagram), pre-classifies each post (sentiment + 9-category da'wah relevance + opportunity score), then clusters into themes. The analysis is done before you ask — you start with situational awareness, not a blank query.
Landing.why_llm_row3_llm: No sharia guardrails. Can write content that contradicts established ijma' or uses framing insensitive to a Muslim audience.
Landing.why_llm_row3_us: Every output labelled 'AI-assisted, not an authoritative fatwa'. Daleel is never generated. Tone: rahma + hikmah, not confrontational/sectarian (PRD §12).
Landing.why_llm_row4_llm: You prompt from scratch every time — guessing what's hot, building context, fact-checking references one by one.
Landing.why_llm_row4_us: Every post comes pre-classified: sentiment, 9 da'wah categories, da'wah-opportunity score. You start with situational awareness, not a blank page.
Landing.why_llm_footnote: TL;DR: use a general LLM for exploring ideas. Use Dakwah-Lens when you need real-time Indonesian media signal + verifiable daleel + sharia guardrails.

Daleel.title: The da'wah Allah and His Messenger call us to
Daleel.subtitle: Dakwah-Lens is built to help da'i fulfill this trust — conveying truth in the best manner to the most receptive audience.
Daleel.verse_1_arabic: ٱدْعُ إِلَىٰ سَبِيلِ رَبِّكَ بِٱلْحِكْمَةِ وَٱلْمَوْعِظَةِ ٱلْحَسَنَةِ ۖ وَجَـٰدِلْهُم بِٱلَّتِى هِىَ أَحْسَنُ
Daleel.verse_1_translation: Invite to the way of your Lord with wisdom and good instruction, and argue with them in a way that is best.
Daleel.verse_1_note: Hikmah, gentle methodology, and the most ihsan dialogue — three principles that guide every Dakwah-Lens brief.
Daleel.verse_2_arabic: وَلْتَكُن مِّنكُمْ أُمَّةٌ يَدْعُونَ إِلَى ٱلْخَيْرِ وَيَأْمُرُونَ بِٱلْمَعْرُوفِ وَيَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ ٱلْمُنكَرِ
Daleel.verse_2_translation: Let there be from among you a community inviting to all that is good, enjoining what is right and forbidding what is wrong.
Daleel.verse_2_note: Da'wah is a collective trust. We strive to make it easier for the community of da'i to carry it.
Daleel.verse_3_arabic: فَوَاللَّهِ لَأَنْ يَهْدِيَ ٱللَّهُ بِكَ رَجُلًا وَاحِدًا خَيْرٌ لَكَ مِنْ حُمْرِ ٱلنَّعَمِ
Daleel.verse_3_translation: By Allah, if Allah guides through you a single man, it is better for you than red camels (the most prized possession).
Daleel.verse_3_note: Guidance for one soul is worth more than all worldly wealth. That is the value behind every brief we help you craft.

Kitab.id_translation_notice: The Qur'an and Sahih Muslim are available in Bahasa Indonesia (manual translation). The remaining hadith collections (Bukhari, Riyad as-Salihin, Bulugh al-Maram) and Tafsir Ibn Kathir are still English-only — we're curating Indonesian translations for them.
Kitab.title: Every citation, traceable to its source
Kitab.body: Each citation in a Dakwah-Lens brief comes from a real, verified kitab — never invented by AI. This is the cornerstone of the trust we work to earn.
Kitab.trust_note: The AI never invents Islamic references. It can only retrieve from this library and always cite back to source.
Kitab.method_1_body: We start with authentic, widely-accepted texts — the Qur'an, major hadith collections, classical tafsir (verse-by-verse commentary), and classical fiqh works. Our Islamic Content Specialist reviews every source before it joins the library.
Kitab.method_2_body: Each kitab is split into small, meaningful sections — a single ayah, one hadith, a paragraph of tafsir or fiqh — so the AI can find exactly the relevant passage instead of pulling a whole book.
Kitab.method_3_body: Every piece is converted into a mathematical representation that captures its meaning. This lets the AI find related passages even when the wording differs.
Kitab.method_4_body: When a brief is generated, the AI looks up the most relevant pieces and quotes them with full attribution — kitab name, verse number, hadith number, or tafsir reference — so you can verify everything yourself.
Kitab.library_subtitle: Starting library for the prototype. More kitabs will be added by our Islamic Content Specialist.
Kitab.kitab_tafsir_meta: Hafiz Ibn Kathir · all 6,236 ayat covered · Mubarakpuri abridged edition

Briefs.placeholder_note: Generated by a deterministic placeholder, not a real LLM. Set ANTHROPIC_API_KEY in .env to enable Claude.
Briefs.wizard_subtitle: Tell us the topic, the audience, and the tone — we'll draft a starter brief grounded in real Qur'anic daleel.
Briefs.field_topic_hint: What conversation do you want to address?
Briefs.field_format_hint: Pick the output style. Friday Khutbah follows the weekly briefing rules (Khutbah Pertama + Kedua, harakat-marked Arabic, traditional Arabic closing du'a).
Briefs.field_current_topic_hint: Optional — anchor the brief to this week's actual public conversation.
Briefs.field_current_topic_checkbox_hint: When ticked, pick one topic from the dropdown — the brief title autofills and the LLM uses real headlines from this week as concrete examples.
Briefs.field_include_profile_hint: Hover to see what gets included.
Briefs.field_include_profile_tooltip: Your onboarding profile (honorific, location, profession, main audience, da'wah focus) is used to tailor examples, framing, and tone. Turn off for a neutral, un-personalized output.
Briefs.field_extra_context_hint: Optional notes for THIS brief — format, recent events, misconceptions to address.
Briefs.field_extra_context_placeholder: e.g. This is for Friday khutbah next week, ~15 minutes. Audience just experienced widespread layoffs at the city's largest factory.
Briefs.preset_khutbah_jumat_value: Format: Friday khutbah (~15 minutes). Open with khutbatul hajah, structure as 2–3 main points with daleel, close with du'a.
Briefs.preset_counter_misconception_value: Address a common misconception about this topic — be specific. Correct with rahma (mercy), never with shaming or sectarian framing.
Briefs.preset_current_events_value: Connect the topic to a recent Indonesian news event the audience has likely seen this week. Keep references general — no naming specific public figures.
Briefs.preset_social_video_value: Output should also work as a 60-second TikTok/Reels script: strong hook in the first 3 seconds, quotable lines, clear call-to-action at the end.
Briefs.preset_action_steps_value: Emphasize 3 concrete, actionable steps the audience can take this week. Avoid vague advice like 'pray more' — give specific behaviors.
Briefs.preset_for_youth_value: Use Gen-Z friendly examples and casual register. Tie to relatable struggles — career anxiety, social-media pressure, identity questions.
Briefs.estimate_body: This is the projected cost before we hit the AI provider. Actual cost may differ slightly (±20%) depending on response length. You'll see the actual cost after generation completes.
Briefs.estimate_disclaimer: Computed against {provider} ({model}) rates. The actual provider may fall back if the primary is unavailable.
Briefs.error_auth_required: Please sign in to generate a brief.
Briefs.error_account_not_approved: Your account is still pending review.
Briefs.error_topic_too_short: Topic must be at least 4 characters.
Briefs.error_extra_context_too_long: Extra context must be 2000 characters or fewer.
Briefs.error_llm_unavailable: Brief generation is temporarily unavailable. The team has been notified.
Briefs.error_retrieval_unavailable: Daleel retrieval is temporarily unavailable — every brief must cite the kitab corpus, so we can't generate one right now. Please try again in a few minutes.
Briefs.error_weak_relevance: We couldn't find strong scriptural support for this topic in the kitab corpus. Try a broader framing or a related theme — for example, swap a very specific term for the underlying concept it touches.
Briefs.error_generation_failed: We couldn't generate this brief — please try again in a moment.
Briefs.error_generic: Could not generate the brief. Please try again.

Insights.segment_spiritual_desc: Aqidah and akhlaq discourse — what shapes how Muslims are thinking and behaving. Best fit for a kajian on character, faith, and daily conduct.
Insights.segment_family_desc: Marriage, parenting, household life, plus physical and mental health through an Islamic lens. The themes a kajian for Muslim families or working women would lean into.
Insights.segment_youth_desc: Student life, identity, mental health, and schooling. The frame for da'wah aimed at Gen Z and young learners.
Insights.segment_justice_desc: Social justice, corruption, economic ethics, layoffs, pinjol/paylater/riba, halal industry — muamalah issues increasingly read as justice issues by Indonesian audiences. Closely tracks what activists and organizers respond to.

Insights.brief_download_print_hint: For a clean output, in the print dialog open 'More settings' and uncheck 'Headers and footers'.
Insights.brief_lang_fallback_body: We currently generate weekly briefings only in Indonesian to keep operating costs sustainable. If you'd like an English version, please reach out via /contact — we'll prioritize it if there's demand.
Insights.brief_nav_body: Compare perspectives across segments in this edition, or jump to a previous edition.
Insights.brief_poster_section_body: One provocative question to grab attention on a campus departmental noticeboard. Anyone who scans the QR can read the article and join the open discussion — share your thought, agree or push back, we don't judge. When a room gets hot, our admins can escalate to an offline meet-up via a direct invitation in the discussion thread.
Insights.brief_flyer_section_body: Six share-ready flyers, each carrying a different angle on the week — including a timely sunnah invitation and one du'a you can recite right away. Download, post to IG / WA story / community group.
Insights.brief_flyer_general_a_body: Khutbah tagline + concrete action steps. Fits personal stories, adult kajian groups.
Insights.brief_flyer_general_b_body: Campaign name + small-action framing for RT / masjid. Fits youth groups, takmir, neighbourhood admins.
Insights.brief_flyer_modern_a_body: Punchy slogan + short message. Scroll-stopping for IG/TikTok feeds aimed at younger audiences.
Insights.brief_flyer_modern_b_body: A reflective question + closing from the open discussion. Good for Twitter/X captions, group discussion posts.
Insights.brief_flyer_sunnah_a_body: The sunnah most timely for this week (Dzulhijjah fast, Mon-Thu fast, dawn sadaqah, etc.) + a short du'a. Good for family WA groups and mosque jamaah.
Insights.brief_flyer_sunnah_b_body: One authentic du'a from Qur'an / Hadith / morning-evening adhkar, tied to this week's theme. Ready to be made a personal wird.
Insights.digest_prompt_title: Get this in your inbox every week?
Insights.digest_prompt_body: The same executive briefing plus the week's headline numbers, delivered Thursday evening WIB — a day before Friday khutbah. One-click unsubscribe in every email.

Insights.cta_body: Free with sign-up. Briefly reviewed by our team to maintain content quality.

About.hero_title: A small halaqoh, a long-term niyah.
About.hero_subtitle: We are a circle of friends — academics by training, professionals by trade — quietly building tools so that da'wah in Indonesia can be sharper, gentler, and more grounded in real daleel.
About.story_title: From a weekly halaqoh, to ship something useful.
About.story_p1: Sukses & Berkah Group started, as many good things do, around a weekly halaqoh. A handful of us — colleagues, classmates, and friends — who kept showing up to study, kept asking each other the same question: what can we actually leave behind?
About.story_p2: Most of the group are active in academia. A few of us work in IT, software engineering, and data science. Different rooms, same conviction: that the tools coming out of the modern Internet — large language models, vector search, classifiers — can serve the ummah, but only if someone with adab and ilm shapes how they're used. Otherwise the technology happens to us, not for us.
About.story_p3: We are deliberately small. We are not a company, not a startup, not a media organization. We are friends who agreed to build slowly, ask our teacher before we publish, and treat every reference we surface as something that will be read by a real da'i preparing for a real audience. That weight is the whole point.
About.supervisor_title: Under the guidance of our teacher.
About.supervisor_role: Professor of Mechanical Engineering, Universitas Indonesia · Advisor, Sukses & Berkah Group
About.supervisor_body: Our group sits under the supervision of Prof. Nasruddin — academic, engineer, and the elder whose questions keep us honest. He is the reason we don't ship before we listen, and the reason every output here is reviewed against the kitab before it goes out the door.
About.supervisor_profile_link: View official UI Faculty of Engineering profile
About.legacy_title: We want a legacy that outlives the keystrokes.
About.legacy_body: Every line of code, every model we train, every Qur'anic passage we embed — we hope it counts as amal jariyah. Not amal that ends when we stop typing, not influence that fades when a feed scrolls past. We are building, in sha Allah, ilm yantafa'u bihi — knowledge from which benefit is taken — for da'i across Indonesia and the diaspora who carry this responsibility every Friday, every halaqoh, every conversation with someone who needs a softer answer.
About.daleel_title: Why amal jariyah, and why "knowledge that benefits".
About.daleel_subtitle: Three foundational references that shape how we measure our own work — not as numbers, but as something that continues after.
About.daleel_1_arabic: إِذَا مَاتَ ٱلْإِنْسَانُ ٱنْقَطَعَ عَنْهُ عَمَلُهُ إِلَّا مِنْ ثَلَاثَةٍ: صَدَقَةٍ جَارِيَةٍ، أَوْ عِلْمٍ يُنْتَفَعُ بِهِ، أَوْ وَلَدٍ صَالِحٍ يَدْعُو لَهُ
About.daleel_1_translation: When a person dies, his deeds come to an end except for three: continuous charity, knowledge from which benefit is gained, or a righteous child who supplicates for him.
About.daleel_1_note: The verse that names our north star. Building tools that help a da'i deliver one better khutbah falls — we hope — under ilmun yuntafa'u bihi.
About.daleel_2_arabic: يَرْفَعِ ٱللَّهُ ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مِنكُمْ وَٱلَّذِينَ أُوتُوا۟ ٱلْعِلْمَ دَرَجَـٰتٍ
About.daleel_2_translation: Allah will raise those who believe among you, and those who have been given knowledge, in degrees.
About.daleel_2_note: Why we are an academic group first. Knowledge is not decoration — it is, by the Qur'an's own framing, the thing that raises a person.
About.daleel_3_translation: The best of you is the one who learns the Qur'an and teaches it.
About.daleel_3_note: Teaching includes the infrastructure that lets the teacher reach further. Our work is, at most, a small contribution to the second half of that sentence.
About.cta_title: Du'a, feedback, or collaboration — we welcome all three.
About.cta_body: If anything we ship benefits even one da'i preparing to deliver their next message, we count this project as more than worth its weight. Pray for us. Tell us where we got it wrong. Help us where you can.

Terms.hero_title: An understanding, more than a contract.
Terms.hero_subtitle: We wrote these in plain language because that's how trust between people of adab actually works. Read them slowly — they're short, and they matter.
Terms.preamble_lede: First, the truth about who's writing this and why.
Terms.preamble_p1: Dakwah-Lens is built by Sukses & Berkah Group — a small circle of friends from a weekly halaqoh, mostly academics, some working in IT and data science. It is not a company. There is no investor. There is no commercial roadmap.
Terms.preamble_p2: Everything you read, click, and generate here is a non-profit effort, offered freely, in the hope that it counts as amal jariyah for everyone who builds it and everyone who uses it well.
Terms.preamble_p3: If you want the longer story, including who supervises this work, it lives on the <about>about page</about>. The terms below are what we ask in return for the trust you place in us.
Terms.s1_body: Dakwah-Lens is a free, non-profit tool for Indonesian da'i — preachers, teachers, content creators, and lifelong students of Islamic knowledge. The app helps you see what your ummah is talking about, retrieves grounded Qur'an and hadith references, and drafts briefs you can adapt. The goal, simply, is to help good people deliver da'wah that is more relevant, more rahma, and more rooted in the kitab.
Terms.s2_title: What you may use it for.
Terms.s2_item_1: Personal study, khutbah preparation, and writing your own kajian / kelas / podcast scripts.
Terms.s2_item_2: Non-commercial da'wah by your masjid, pesantren, ta'lim circle, or online community.
Terms.s2_item_3: Non-monetized social media content — videos, posts, threads — provided you treat the AI output as a starting draft, not the final word.
Terms.s2_item_4: Sharing, quoting, and adapting the daleel we surface (it's not ours; it belongs to the ummah).
Terms.s3_title: What we ask you not to do.
Terms.s3_item_1: Don't resell access, package this app as a paid service, or feed it into a course/product you charge people for. The work is offered freely; please pass it on freely.
Terms.s3_item_2: Don't use Dakwah-Lens to attack other Muslims, push sectarian or politically-aligned messaging, or contradict established ijma' on fundamental aqidah.
Terms.s3_item_3: Don't pass off AI-generated briefs as your own scholarly work. Always do your own review before delivering anything to an audience.
Terms.s3_item_4: Don't scrape the app, automate sign-ups, or otherwise burn through our limited budget — this is a small project running on a small VPS funded by the team and donors.
Terms.s3_item_5: Don't use it for anything haram, illegal under Indonesian law, or anything that violates the hard rules our supervisor would not approve.
Terms.s4_title: About the AI — and the limits of it.
Terms.s4_p1: Every Qur'an and hadith reference you see in a brief is retrieved by semantic search from real kitab — never invented by the language model. That is the single most important architectural rule of this project. If we ever produce a daleel that you cannot verify against its named source, please report it immediately.
Terms.s4_p2: Everything else in a brief — analysis, audience framing, khutbah outline, social caption — is generated by an AI. The AI is a junior assistant working under your supervision, not a scholar. It mirrors your input, including the audience and tone you select, and including your own biases. Treat its output as a draft to be reviewed, sharpened, and approved.
Terms.s4_callout: AI-assisted. Not an authoritative fatwa. The religious responsibility for anything you say publicly remains yours, in front of Allah ﷻ.
Terms.s5_p1: We do our best to keep the app up, the daleel accurate, and the classifier honest. We cannot guarantee any of it. Models change. Outlets change their RSS. Bugs happen. The app might be slow, down, or wrong on any given day.
Terms.s5_p2: Sukses & Berkah Group, our supervisor Prof. Nasruddin, and everyone contributing to this project bear no legal liability for content you create, audiences you address, or decisions you make based on what you saw here. By using Dakwah-Lens, you accept that the consequences of your da'wah are yours alone.
Terms.s6_title: Your data — what we keep and why.
Terms.s6_p1: We store the minimum needed for the app to work: your account, your generated briefs, anonymous page-view counts for our analytics, and any cost metadata we need to stay under the donor-funded budget. We do not sell, rent, or share your data with any third party. We host inside Indonesia per UU PDP No. 27/2022 §17.
Terms.s6_p2: If you want to delete your account, tell us — we will. The longer write-up of what we collect lives on the <privacy>privacy page</privacy>.
Terms.s7_body: We may update these terms as the project evolves. Trivial copy edits will happen quietly. Anything material — a change in scope, in data practices, or in what counts as acceptable use — we will announce by email to signed-in users and post a banner in-app for at least two weeks. The version date at the top of this page always tells you when we last touched it.
Terms.s8_body: We are a small group, not a support desk — but we read everything. The best way to reach us is through the contact information on the <contact>Contact</contact>. Disputes that cannot be resolved by good-faith conversation are governed by the law of the Republic of Indonesia.
Terms.closing_body: We ask Allah to accept this work as a small amal jariyah, to forgive what we got wrong, and to bless anyone — user, contributor, donor, du'a maker — who carries it further than we could.

Privacy.hero_title: What we keep, what we never keep.
Privacy.hero_subtitle: Privacy as adab. We collect what the app actually needs, name it plainly, and host it in Indonesia. Nothing leaves this building.
Privacy.preamble_lede: The short version, before the details.
Privacy.preamble_p1: Dakwah-Lens is built and run by Sukses & Berkah Group, a small non-profit team. We don't sell data. We don't share it with any advertiser. We don't embed third-party trackers in your browser. The team uses your data only to run the app — and we keep as little of it as we honestly can.
Privacy.preamble_p2: Your account, your briefs, and your onboarding profile are yours. You can read them, change them, and ask us to delete them at any time. The legal framework we operate under is Indonesia's Undang-Undang Pelindungan Data Pribadi (UU PDP) No. 27/2022.
Privacy.preamble_p3: This page covers the privacy promises. Our broader covenant — what the app is for, who builds it, what you may and may not use it for — lives in the <terms>terms page</terms>.
Privacy.s1_p1: Enough to make the app work — nothing decorative. The list below is exhaustive at the time this page was last updated:
Privacy.s1_item_1: Account: your name, email, password (stored as a one-way bcrypt hash — never as plain text), email-verified timestamp, account status (pending / approved / blocked), and role.
Privacy.s1_item_2: Onboarding profile: the answers you gave in the welcome wizard — panggilan, age range, region, role, audience, focus topics, language preference. Used to tailor briefs to your context.
Privacy.s1_item_3: Briefs you generate: topic, segment, tone, locale, the retrieved daleel, and the AI-generated content. These belong to you.
Privacy.s1_item_4: Anonymous page-view rows: which path you visited (locale-stripped, e.g. `/insights/x`, never with query strings), referer, user-agent string, locale, an httpOnly session cookie ID, and your user_id if signed in. Used for analytics; never sold, never enriched.
Privacy.s1_item_5: Cost / usage events: which paid API call was made, how much it cost, when. No user content goes into this table.
Privacy.s1_item_6: If you sign in with Google: the Google-issued account ID, profile name, and avatar URL. We store no Google access tokens beyond what NextAuth needs to keep your session valid.
Privacy.s2_p1: Calling these out explicitly because the usual web defaults capture them, and we don't:
Privacy.s2_item_1: Your IP address. We never log it, never store it in a database column, never pass it to a third party.
Privacy.s2_item_2: Fingerprints (canvas, WebGL, font lists, audio context — any of the techniques used to identify you across sites). No third-party JavaScript SDK runs on Dakwah-Lens.
Privacy.s2_item_3: Precise location — no GPS. What we keep: the broad region you self-declared in onboarding, plus a coarse region code (e.g. province) derived from your IP solely for anonymous visitor analytics — the IP itself is never stored.
Privacy.s2_item_4: Anything you write in a brief draft and don't save. Drafts that aren't submitted never reach our database.
Privacy.s3_p1: Both are first-party. There are no third-party cookies. There is no marketing cookie consent banner because there is nothing to consent to beyond these:
Privacy.s3_cookie_1_purpose: Your signed-in session. Set by Auth.js when you log in, deleted when you log out. Without this, you can't be signed in.
Privacy.s3_cookie_2_purpose: A random UUID, httpOnly, lasting 1 year. Used to count distinct visitor sessions in our analytics without knowing who you are. Cannot be read by JavaScript, cannot be linked to your identity unless you sign in.
Privacy.s3_footer: That's it. No Google Analytics. No Meta pixel. No Hotjar. No advertising cookies.
Privacy.s4_p1: To do its job, the app sends specific data to a small set of services. What each one sees is listed plainly:
Privacy.s4_p_openai_body: The topic text of a brief, sent for embedding (so we can find relevant Qur'anic verses). No user account info goes with it.
Privacy.s4_p_gemini_body: Social-media post text (for the relevance classifier) and brief topics + retrieved daleel (for synthesis). The automated weekly briefings include no account info. When you (signed-in) generate your own brief, your profile — preferred title, region, audience, da'wah focus — is also sent so the draft can be tailored.
Privacy.s4_p_anthropic_body: Brief topics + retrieved daleel, only when Gemini is unavailable (fallback). When you generate your own brief, your profile is sent the same way it is to Gemini.
Privacy.s4_p_resend_body: Your email address + the verification/reset email content. Sent only when you sign up or request a password reset.
Privacy.s4_p_google_body: Only if you choose Google sign-in. Google handles authentication; we receive your email + name + avatar back.
Privacy.s4_p_apify_body: Hashtag and keyword queries we run to fetch public posts. Never any of your personal data.
Privacy.s4_p_youtube_body: Search queries we run to fetch public da'wah-related videos. Never any of your personal data.
Privacy.s4_footer: Every provider above is bound by their own privacy policy. We never send them your account-level identity unless their service requires it (Resend, Google OAuth). Cost-tracking metadata for each call lives in our usage_events table; none of it includes your data, only the API call shape.
Privacy.s5_p1: All databases (Postgres + Qdrant + Redis) are hosted inside Indonesia. Specifically, at IDCloudHost VPS facilities in Jakarta. This is a deliberate choice per UU PDP No. 27/2022 §17 — Indonesian personal data should stay in Indonesia unless there is a specific cross-border arrangement.
Privacy.s5_p2: The third-party services in the previous section process data on their own infrastructure (which is typically outside Indonesia — OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Resend are mostly US-based). They receive the minimum needed to do their job, never your account identity unless required, and never your full database row.
Privacy.s6_title: Your rights — what you can ask us to do.
Privacy.s6_p1: Per UU PDP No. 27/2022 §6, you have specific rights over your own data, and we honor every one of them without arguing:
Privacy.s6_item_1: Access — see everything we have on you in human-readable form.
Privacy.s6_item_2: Correction — fix anything wrong. You can edit most of it yourself; for what you can't, email us.
Privacy.s6_item_3: Deletion — wipe your account and every row tied to it, full stop. We will keep aggregated analytics rows that have already been de-identified.
Privacy.s6_item_4: Portability — export your briefs, your onboarding profile, your usage history in JSON. Just ask.
Privacy.s6_item_5: Restriction — temporarily disable processing while we sort out a dispute, without losing the data.
Privacy.s6_p2: To exercise any of these, reach us via the contact information on the <about>about page</about>. We aim to respond within 7 working days.
Privacy.s7_item_1: Account + profile + briefs: until you delete them, or until you ask us to delete the whole account.
Privacy.s7_item_2: Page-view rows: 18 months, then truncated. You can ask us to scrub yours sooner.
Privacy.s7_item_3: Email verification + password-reset tokens: deleted on use, or after 24 hours / 1 hour respectively (whichever comes first).
Privacy.s7_item_4: Cost / usage events: kept indefinitely for budgeting and audit, but contains no personal data — only API metadata.
Privacy.s8_body: Dakwah-Lens is meant for da'i and adult lifelong learners. We do not knowingly collect data from anyone under 13 years old. If you believe a minor has signed up, tell us — we will delete the account.
Privacy.s9_body: We may update this policy as the app evolves. Material changes — any change in what we collect, share, or keep — we will announce by email to signed-in users and post an in-app banner for at least two weeks before they take effect. The date at the top always tells you when we last touched this. The <terms>terms page</terms> announcement policy follows the same rules.
Privacy.closing_translation: Indeed, Allah is appreciative and forbearing.
Privacy.closing_body: We treat the data you trust to us the way we'd want our own treated — quietly, minimally, and with the seriousness of an amanah. If you find a place where we fell short, please tell us.

Transparency.hero_title: Where every rupiah comes from and goes to.
Transparency.hero_subtitle: Sukses & Berkah Group is a small non-profit. We publish what we receive, what we spend, and where the difference sits. Updated whenever a new donation or invoice lands.
Transparency.fx_title: About the USD → IDR conversion you see here.
Transparency.fx_rate_line: We display all USD-billed costs (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, Apify, Resend, YouTube) at a fixed rate of 1 USD = Rp {rate} (last updated {updated}).
Transparency.fx_caveat: Because the real USD/IDR exchange rate fluctuates daily, the rupiah figures on this page are illustrative — the precise IDR each provider actually charges depends on the card-network rate at billing time. Manual cost rows (VPS, domain) are entered in IDR directly, so those are exact.
Transparency.prepaid_body: Several of our providers are prepaid — we have to top up a balance before we can consume any of it. IDCloudHost (VPS), Apify (scrapers), OpenAI (embeddings), and Anthropic Claude (brief synthesis) all work this way. What we record as a cost here is the top-up amount, not what's actually been consumed. Real usage is almost always lower — unused balance just sits in each provider's account until later runs draw it down. Your donations therefore last longer than the spend numbers on this page suggest.
Transparency.monthly_subtitle: Donations in green, total spend in slate, net balance on the right. Everything converted to IDR using the rate noted above.
Transparency.recent_donations_subtitle: The last 12 receipts. Donors who asked to remain anonymous are shown as such — we never publish a name that wasn't consented to.
Transparency.no_donations_yet: No donations recorded yet. When the first one lands, it shows up here.
Transparency.closing_body: Whether through du'a, sharing the app with one da'i you know, or a transfer — every contribution helps keep this small non-profit running.

HowItWorks.page_title: How Dakwah-Lens works — technical deep dive
HowItWorks.badge: For engineers, students, and the curious
HowItWorks.hero_title: Under the hood: how Dakwah-Lens is built.
HowItWorks.hero_subtitle: A non-trivial AI stack delivered as a small, well-bounded monorepo. This page walks through every layer — services, models, data flow, and the trade-offs behind them.
HowItWorks.tldr_arch_body: Monorepo — Next.js 16 frontend + FastAPI/Celery backend sharing one Postgres. Qdrant holds the kitab embeddings; Redis brokers Celery.
HowItWorks.tldr_ai_body: Provider-agnostic. Gemini Pro for synthesis · Claude Sonnet 4.5 fallback · Gemini Flash-Lite for sentiment + relevance · Gemini for topic discovery.
HowItWorks.tldr_trust_body: Every Qur'an / hadith reference is retrieved from the corpus — never invented by the LLM.
HowItWorks.stack_subtitle: Five layers — UI, application, data, AI, infrastructure. Each box names the library + what it does so you can dive into the source by name.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_nextjs: App Router, server components, server actions. Renders SSR for SEO and the marketing pages.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_react: Concurrent rendering enables the route-level loading.tsx fallbacks.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_tailwind: Utility CSS, no design system framework — keeps the bundle thin.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_intl: i18n for `id` (default) + `en`. All copy lives in /messages/*.json.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_auth: Email/password + Google OAuth. JWT session strategy with email verification gate.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_drizzle: TS-side ORM. Mirrors the SQLAlchemy schema for type-safe reads.
HowItWorks.stack_frontend_lucide: Single icon set, kept consistent across the app.
HowItWorks.stack_backend_fastapi: Async API surface for the FE + scripts CLI entry points.
HowItWorks.stack_backend_alembic: Schema migrations — single source of truth.
HowItWorks.stack_backend_celery: Scheduled ingest, classification batches, topic clustering, system metrics.
HowItWorks.stack_backend_uv: Fast deps + virtualenv via Astral's uv. Replaces poetry/pip.
HowItWorks.stack_backend_pydantic: Settings + request/response models; configuration via env.
HowItWorks.stack_data_pg: Primary OLTP — users, social_posts, briefs, every observability table.
HowItWorks.stack_data_qdrant: Vector DB. Holds the kitab corpus embeddings (~6,236 ayah + hadith).
HowItWorks.stack_ai_openai: text-embedding-3-large for embedding the kitab corpus (Qur'an, hadith, tafsir) and matching brief-topic queries against it via Qdrant.
HowItWorks.stack_ai_gemini: Pro for brief synthesis + weekly briefings; Flash-Lite for sentiment + relevance + topic discovery + daleel reranking + comment moderation.
HowItWorks.stack_ai_anthropic: Brief synthesis fallback when Gemini is unavailable.
HowItWorks.stack_ai_trafilatura: Article body extraction for RSS feeds with thin ledes.
HowItWorks.stack_infra_docker: Local dev: docker compose up brings Postgres + Qdrant + Redis.
HowItWorks.stack_infra_vps: Hosted in Jakarta per UU PDP §17 data residency.
HowItWorks.stack_infra_youtube: Direct YouTube Data API (cheaper than Apify YouTube actors).
HowItWorks.system_subtitle: Four tiers, each with one job. The Celery worker is a first-class client of the data layer — it speaks Postgres directly, like the web app does.
HowItWorks.ingest_subtitle: Five stages. Each beat tick fans out N keywords as independent child tasks — failure isolation per keyword, not just per platform.
HowItWorks.ingest_stage_1_body: Five platforms: X / Instagram / TikTok via Apify, YouTube via the Data API, mainstream RSS via feedparser + trafilatura for full-body extraction. All return a unified `ScrapeResult` shape.
HowItWorks.ingest_stage_2_body: Per-platform normalizer maps raw payloads → SocialPost row dicts. Author handle extraction, URL truncation, posted_at parsing, region tagging for RSS.
HowItWorks.ingest_stage_3_body: Three parallel Gemini Flash-Lite passes — (1) event-valence sentiment across every platform (unified since IndoBERT was retired 2026-05-25), (2) 9-category da'wah relevance, (3) focused da'wah-opportunity score. Cost: ~$0.0003 per post.
HowItWorks.ingest_stage_4_body: Conflict on (platform, external_id) → update text + classifications + region. Re-running a scrape never produces duplicates.
HowItWorks.ingest_stage_5_body: Daily Gemini Flash-Lite topic discovery per platform, fired at 04:00 WIB by Celery beat. Reads every post from the last 7 days above a da'wah-opportunity floor (typically 300–600 mainstream posts) and asks Gemini to identify 6–10 themes with human-readable Bahasa Indonesia labels framed from a da'wah angle, then writes them to `topics`.
HowItWorks.ingest_schedule_note: Scheduled via Celery beat (Asia/Jakarta): mainstream RSS every 2 hours · X weekly every Wednesday 22:00 WIB (500-item cap/keyword, 7-day window) · TikTok weekly Wednesday 22:10 WIB and Instagram weekly Wednesday 22:20 WIB (limit 20 items/keyword, evaluation phase since 2026-05-25) · YouTube whitelist channel sweep weekly every Wednesday 21:00 WIB (Data API uploads path — trusted da'i voices) · daily trending overlay at 12:00 WIB: da'wah-relevant keywords scraped on X (apidojo) + unbounded YouTube search (Data API search.list, with a language gate) · Gemini topic discovery at 04:00 WIB daily (mainstream + YouTube) · X + TikTok + Instagram topic discovery every Thursday 04:00 WIB · insights briefings every Thursday 05:00 WIB — one day before the Friday khutbah (currently paused for cost, triggered manually via Claude) · email digest every Thursday 08:00 WIB · Apify billing reconcile at 06:00 WIB daily. Manual runs via `uv run python -m api.scripts.ingest`.
HowItWorks.discovery_title: Keyword discovery: curated rotation + trending overlay.
HowItWorks.discovery_subtitle: Two complementary layers. The weekly curated sweep guarantees structural coverage across the 9 da'wah categories; the daily trending overlay catches today's named events the curated list could never anticipate.
HowItWorks.discovery_curated_body: ~49 keywords across 9 categories (religious, family, youth, muamalah, social justice, education, health, current events, cultural), admin-editable at /admin/system/queries. CURRENTLY ACTIVE: mainstream RSS (every 2h via feedparser, no keywords needed) and X (apidojo, $0.0004/item, weekly every Wednesday 22:00 WIB, 500-item cap/keyword + 7-day window). 1-WEEK EVALUATION PHASE since 2026-05-25: TikTok (clockworks/free-tiktok-scraper, $0.004/item, weekly Wednesday 22:10 WIB, limit 20 items/keyword) and Instagram (apify/instagram-hashtag-scraper, $0.0023/item, weekly Wednesday 22:20 WIB, limit 20 items/keyword) — cost and sentiment accuracy assessed after the first run. YOUTUBE (since 2026-05-27): verified-channel whitelist sweep weekly every Wednesday 21:00 WIB (free Data API uploads path — the "trusted da'i voices" half of the YouTube split); needs a one-time channel seed + admin verify. All code paths wired.
HowItWorks.discovery_trending_body: LIVE since 2026-05-25 — re-enabled alongside the weekly X scraper. Daily 12:00 WIB, three free official sources merge: Google Trends Indonesia RSS, YouTube Data API mostPopular regionCode=ID, Google News Indonesia RSS. A single Gemini Flash-Lite call extracts a short search keyword from each candidate AND judges social-situation relevance (KEEP if it touches society/policy/family/youth/economy/etc., SKIP only purely-entertainment chatter) in one shot. The surviving keywords (up to 8) get ad-hoc scraped on X (apidojo) and searched on YouTube unbounded by channel (Data API search.list, with a language gate to block cross-language spam) — the "whatever is spiking" half of the YouTube split. Marginal cost ~$1/mo (YouTube free within quota).
HowItWorks.discovery_why_note: Why two layers: curated alone leaves 7–25 day refresh gaps per keyword — fine for evergreen topics (riba, ibadah) but blind to a viral incident breaking that morning. Pure trending alone over-indexes on entertainment (K-pop fandom, sports). The hybrid covers both.
HowItWorks.platforms_subtitle: The collection strategy differs per platform — tuned to cost, rate-limits, and signal quality. Here's the exact method for each.
HowItWorks.platforms_rss_body: NO keywords. We read the FULL RSS feeds of ~27 Indonesian outlets (Kompas, Detik, Antara, Republika, Tempo, CNN, etc.) every 2 hours, then extract full article text via feedparser + trafilatura. Free, no Apify — the primary, densest source.
HowItWorks.platforms_x_body: KEYWORD-based. A pool of ~49 da'wah-relevant keywords (superadmin-editable) is scraped weekly every Wednesday 22:00 WIB via Apify (apidojo), with a 500-item cap/keyword and a 7-day window. Plus da'wah-relevant trending keywords from the daily 12:00 WIB overlay.
HowItWorks.platforms_tiktok_body: KEYWORD-based. The same keyword pool is scraped weekly Wednesday 22:10 WIB via Apify (clockworks/free-tiktok-scraper), limit 20 items/keyword. In an evaluation phase since 2026-05-25.
HowItWorks.platforms_ig_body: HASHTAG-based. The keyword pool maps to hashtags, scraped weekly Wednesday 22:20 WIB via Apify (instagram-hashtag-scraper), limit 20 items/keyword. In an evaluation phase.
HowItWorks.platforms_yt_body: (1) WEEKLY, CHANNEL-based: a whitelist of verified da'i channels has its latest uploads pulled every Wednesday 21:00 WIB via the YouTube Data API (cheap uploads path) — the "trusted da'i voices" layer. (2) DAILY, SEARCH-based: da'wah-relevant trending keywords are searched across ALL of YouTube (search.list, unbounded by channel) on the 12:00 WIB overlay, with a language gate to block cross-language spam — the "whatever is spiking" layer. Both are free within the Data API quota.
HowItWorks.platforms_note: Mainstream RSS is the backbone (free, dense); paid-Apify social platforms run weekly on keywords/hashtags to keep cost down; YouTube uses the free Data API with two paths at once.
HowItWorks.platforms_faq_q_low_count: Why are post counts from Instagram & TikTok much lower than other platforms?
HowItWorks.platforms_faq_a_low_count_cost: The main reason is cost. Mainstream RSS is free (we pull every 2 hours), YouTube Data API is also free, and X is relatively cheap (~$0.0004 per post). But Instagram (~$0.0023 per post via apify/instagram-hashtag-scraper) and TikTok (~$0.004 per post via clockworks/free-tiktok-scraper) charge per item, and our Apify budget is $29/month. That's why we cap items per keyword (IG 60, TT 20) and run weekly on Wednesday nights rather than daily like mainstream RSS.
HowItWorks.platforms_faq_a_low_count_signal: So why scrape them at all if the volume is small? Because IG and TikTok give us a window into a different audience — especially Gen Z. The language patterns, viral issues, content formats, and emotional reactions on TikTok are very different from mainstream news or X (which skew older / news-reader). Even a small sample of ~600–1,500 posts per week is enough to capture signal: which da'wah categories are trending with Gen Z, what misconceptions are emerging, and what content formats work for younger audiences. Without scraping IG/TT, the da'wah briefing skews toward the news-reader audience (mostly 30+ years old).
HowItWorks.brief_subtitle: The hard guarantee: every Qur'an + hadith reference is retrieved from the corpus by semantic search — never invented by the LLM. The LLM only frames the surrounding context.
HowItWorks.brief_stage_1_body: User submits topic + segment + tone + locale + optional extra context. Onboarding profile attached server-side.
HowItWorks.brief_stage_2_body: OpenAI text-embedding-3-large turns the topic into a 3072-dim vector. Enriched with the audience segment label to bias retrieval toward audience-relevant verses, hadith, and tafsir passages.
HowItWorks.brief_stage_3_body: Cosine search across all kitab collections in parallel (~4 candidates per corpus, ~15 total). A second Gemini Flash-Lite pass thematically reranks the pool down to the 5 most relevant — cosine alone surfaces verses that share tokens with the query but miss the actual da'wah angle. If retrieval fails, the brief generator surfaces a typed error to the UI.
HowItWorks.brief_stage_4_body: Gemini Pro (primary) gets the topic, profile, extra context, and retrieved daleel. Falls back to Claude Sonnet 4.5 if Gemini fails. Output is constrained to a JSON schema.
HowItWorks.brief_stage_5_body: Zod-validates the response shape, attaches the retrieved daleel array, persists to `briefs`. Failures surface as a typed error to the form, never a half-baked row.
HowItWorks.brief_callout_body: the system prompt explicitly forbids the LLM from inventing additional Qur'anic verses or hadith citations. The model only sees the daleel array we retrieved — if it tries to add more, validation catches it.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_subtitle: What the system (or operator) writes every Thursday for everyone — 5 briefings per cycle (1 overall view + 4 per-topic segments) in Indonesian. Public at /insights/brief/{date}-{segment}. Each briefing is a full 11,000-14,700-word content kit with 8 ready-to-use deliverables + a campus bulletin-board poster + 6 share-ready flyers.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_stage_1_body: Celery cron is wired for Thursday 05:00 WIB (1h after the 04:00 topic recluster, a day before the Friday khutbah) — but PAUSED since 2026-05-23 to keep LLM cost at zero during the dev phase. Operator currently runs manually via `manual_briefing.py dump <segment>` which runs the data-prep pipeline and emits the prompt — Claude's reply is saved back via `manual_briefing.py save`. Re-enable the cron by uncommenting the block in celery_app.py.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_stage_2_body: Seven-day aggregations from `social_posts`: sentiment mix, top categories with share-pct, top topics joined with sample headlines per topic. When no baseline week exists yet, delta_pp emits null instead of fabricating change. Per-segment briefings filter on dominant-cat above the 0.1 punt floor.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_stage_3_body: Flash-Lite builds a retrieval query from stats. OpenAI embeds it. Qdrant searches all 6 kitab collections (~4 per corpus → 15 candidates). Flash-Lite second pass reranks the pool thematically to the 10 strongest matches. Hadith translation_id is filled in where empty — Quran already has both ID + EN (Kemenag + Sahih International). 10-deep pool (was 5) so each Section-4 sub-section can weave 2-3 inline citations without repeating.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_stage_4_body: Auto mode (cron enabled): Gemini 2.5 Pro called once with max_output 32,768 tokens + 12,288-token thinking budget. Current manual mode: paste prompt + context into Claude (chat), save reply via manual_briefing.py. Output is identical in both modes: 5-section markdown 7,300-9,800 words in Indonesian. Daleel-grounded — model can ONLY cite from the 10-daleel retrieved pool. English version paused since 2026-05-23 (fallback banner shown to EN readers).
HowItWorks.insights_brief_stage_5_body: Writes a row to `insights_summaries` (summary_md, headline_stats, daleel_refs, segment, model, tokens, cost). Public page /insights/brief/{slug} renders with sticky TOC + share/download toolbar. Section 4 is split into 6 interactive deliverable cards (click to open modal). Flyers are rendered on-demand by Puppeteer + HTML/Tailwind — 2 variants (general + Gen-Z) per briefing plus 6 per-deliverable variants. CDN-cached 1h, immutable.
HowItWorks.insights_brief_callout_strong: Same Sharia discipline as user briefs:
HowItWorks.insights_brief_callout_body: the long-form prompt explicitly forbids freelance Quran/hadith citations. Every reference comes from the 10-daleel rerank pool — if the model tries to add more, it's a violation of the prompt rule. Disclaimer baked into Section 5 of every briefing: AI-assisted, not an authoritative fatwa.
HowItWorks.anatomy_subtitle: What lands in the content box every week. One briefing = 5 markdown sections (Section 4 carries 8 separate deliverables) + a campus bulletin-board poster with QR + 6 share-ready flyers (incl. sunnah invitation + recitable du'a).
HowItWorks.anatomy_s1_body: One opening paragraph: top 3 categories with share-pct, sentiment mix, and the week's main themes.
HowItWorks.anatomy_s2_body: Detailed headline stats: per-category volume + delta, sample headlines per topic, week-over-week sentiment shift.
HowItWorks.anatomy_s3_body: Thematic narrative that ties raw headlines into a pattern relevant to this week's da'wah.
HowItWorks.anatomy_s5_body: Bibliography — 8-10 from the 10-deep retrieved pool. Format: bold citation + translation + 1-2 sentences of context. Ends with the AI-assisted disclaimer.
HowItWorks.anatomy_section4_title: Da'wah Strategies & Actions — 8 ready-to-use deliverables in one briefing
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_khutbah_body: From mukadimah, opening verse, 6-9-paragraph body, closing du'a in Arabic transliteration — just adapt to your jamaah.
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_kajian_body: 45-minute format: 3 talking points + real news examples + anticipated Q&A + close.
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_home_body: 3-4 scenarios for parents with kids (SD/SMP/SMA): opening questions + anticipated responses + close.
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_content_body: 60-90 seconds for TikTok/IG Reels/Shorts: 5-second hook → 40-60-second body with 1 daleel → CTA.
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_genz_body: Bulletin-board poster (provocative question + QR) → magazine-style article page with realistic Q&A. Logic-first framing for students who push back on top-down sermons.
HowItWorks.anatomy_kit_action_body: 4 concrete small-budget (<IDR 500K) actions for kids / teens / adults / elderly — runnable in parallel as one RT campaign.
HowItWorks.anatomy_flyer_title: 6 share-ready flyers + 1 campus poster, rendered on-demand
HowItWorks.anatomy_flyer_body: Puppeteer + HTML/Tailwind renders 1080×1080 PNG flyers from the briefing content that already exists. composeFlyer() picks layout + image from the registry deterministically per edition+slot — so share-URLs stay stable but the visual rotates each week.
HowItWorks.anatomy_flyer_general_body: Ayat-centered layout with Amiri calligraphy, 8-point star ornaments, mosque / interior photo as backdrop. Fits mosque groups, adult kajian, personal stories.
HowItWorks.anatomy_flyer_genz_body: Punchy 64-78px headline as the focal element, violet→fuchsia→amber palette, ayat as a tilted supporting card. Designed for IG / TikTok feeds.
HowItWorks.anatomy_flyer_footnote: Plus 6 per-deliverable flyers (khutbah / kajian / home / content / genz / action) shown in the Section-4 card modals. Total 8 flyers × 5 briefings = 40 share-ready PNGs per cycle, all rendered by Puppeteer (no LLM call, no extra cost).
HowItWorks.models_subtitle: Each choice is the smallest model that gets the job done well, not the largest one available.
HowItWorks.models_flash_role: Unified classifier across all platforms since 2026-05-25 (replaces retired IndoBERT): event-valence sentiment + 9-category da'wah relevance (aqidah, akhlaq, muamalah, social_justice, family, youth, education, economic_ethics, health) + opportunity scoring + topic discovery + daleel reranking + discussion comment moderation.
HowItWorks.models_flash_why: Cheapest Gemini tier. Cost per post is ~$0.0003 across all three classifier passes — fits the IDR 1.5-2M/month operating-cost cap with comfortable headroom.
HowItWorks.models_flash_where: Called from api/src/api/services/relevance.py (batches of 10), api/src/api/services/topic_discovery.py, and api/src/api/services/kitab_retrieval.py (rerank).
HowItWorks.models_pro_role: Weekly insights briefings (Thursday 05:00 WIB) — 5 audience segments in Indonesian, ~7,300-9,800-word long-form markdown per briefing, retrieval-grounded daleel. Cron is currently paused for cost; generation is triggered manually via Claude before publish. On-demand admin brief synthesis available as an experimental feature.
HowItWorks.models_pro_why: Top-tier reasoning at ~35% lower cost than Claude Sonnet 4.5. Daily insights briefings cost ~$0.06 per segment-language (~$9/mo for all 5 segments × 2 langs); on-demand user briefs ~$0.016 each.
HowItWorks.models_pro_where: Insights path: api/src/api/services/insights_summary.py. User-brief path: web/src/lib/llm.ts → callGemini().
HowItWorks.models_claude_role: Brief synthesis fallback when Gemini errors out.
HowItWorks.models_claude_why: Different cloud, similar quality bracket. Diversifies single-vendor outage risk.
HowItWorks.models_embedding_role: Topic-text → vector for Qur'an semantic retrieval.
HowItWorks.models_embedding_why: 3072-dim embeddings give noticeably better retrieval than -3-small on classical Arabic translation drift.
HowItWorks.models_embedding_where: Called from web/src/lib/quran-retrieval.ts before each Qdrant query.
HowItWorks.cost_subtitle: Real, current numbers — the platform deliberately runs on a tight budget. Total operating cost (server + API) is capped at IDR 1.5-2M (~$92-123) per month. Schedule and rates as of May 2026.
HowItWorks.cost_budget_caveat: Apify dashboard is the authoritative source; daily reconcile job catches any drift. Brief synthesis (Claude / Gemini Pro) is on-demand only — usage scales with user volume after launch.
HowItWorks.cost_x_use: Scrape X tweets via apidojo/tweet-scraper, weekly Wednesday 22:00 WIB (50 enabled keywords, 500-item cap/keyword, 7-day window).
HowItWorks.cost_ig_use: Scrape Instagram hashtag posts, weekly Wednesday 22:20 WIB (37 enabled hashtags, limit 60 items/keyword as of 2026-05-30).

HowItWorks.cost_rss_use: Mainstream news every 2 hours, no API cost.

HowItWorks.cost_claude_use: Brief synthesis fallback when Gemini Pro errors.

HowItWorks.kitab_title: The kitab corpus — what's embedded in Qdrant.
HowItWorks.kitab_subtitle: v0 corpus per PRD §12. Embedded once, never rewritten by the application — admin-only re-embedding via the embed script.
HowItWorks.kitab_quran: Full 6,236 ayah in Arabic + Indonesian (Kemenag translation) + English (Sahih International). Tagged with topic keywords by Gemini for better retrieval recall.
HowItWorks.kitab_bukhari: Sahih al-Bukhari, full chapter structure with hadith numbers.
HowItWorks.kitab_muslim: Sahih Muslim, full chapter structure with hadith numbers.
HowItWorks.kitab_riyad: Riyad as-Salihin (Imam an-Nawawi). Pedagogical hadith collection, organized by topic.
HowItWorks.kitab_tafsir: Tafsir Ibn Kathir (Hafiz Ibn Kathir) in Arabic + English (Mubarakpuri abridged). Verse-by-verse commentary across all 6,236 ayat — chunked into ~1000-token windows so retrieval finds the exact paragraph, not the whole entry.
HowItWorks.kitab_note: Every entry in Qdrant carries (a) the original Arabic, (b) the translation in the brief's locale, and (c) the canonical citation (book + chapter + ayah/hadith number). Briefs always link back to the named source so the da'i can verify before delivery.
HowItWorks.obs_title: Observability — the boring but important part.
HowItWorks.obs_subtitle: Most of these tables are append-only event logs. They drive the superadmin /admin/system dashboard.
HowItWorks.obs_social_posts: Every scraped item, with classifier output. Indexed on (platform, posted_at), (platform, region), and a unique (platform, external_id) for dedup.
HowItWorks.obs_topics: Theme clusters discovered by Gemini topic discovery per platform — auto-refreshed nightly.
HowItWorks.obs_briefs: User-generated briefs. Stores the retrieved daleel inline (JSONB) so brief reads never re-hit Qdrant.
HowItWorks.obs_usage_events: Every paid API call (OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic, Apify, YouTube, RSS, Resend) with cost + tokens. Drives the API costs dashboard.
HowItWorks.obs_system_metrics: psutil snapshot every 60s. Drives the host CPU / memory / disk charts.
HowItWorks.obs_ingest_runs: start_run / finish_run lifecycle for every Celery task. Drives the pipeline-health dashboard.
HowItWorks.obs_page_views: Anonymous httpOnly session cookie. No IPs, no fingerprints. Drives web analytics.
HowItWorks.obs_costs: Manual VPS / domain invoice entries + recorded donations. Drives the public /transparency page.
HowItWorks.obs_contact: Public /contact form submissions. Forwarded to ADMIN_EMAIL and stored as durable backup.
HowItWorks.obs_auth: NextAuth.js-standard tables. Verification tokens used for email-verification + password-reset flows.
HowItWorks.tradeoffs_title: Choices we made, and what we picked against.
HowItWorks.tradeoffs_subtitle: Tech is full of judgement calls. Here's the explicit version of ours.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_1_q: Why a monorepo with Python + TypeScript instead of a single language?
HowItWorks.tradeoff_1_a: Python wins for the data/ML side (Gemini / OpenAI / Anthropic client integrations, Qdrant client, embedding pipeline — all most mature in Python). Next.js + Auth.js + Drizzle wins for the user-facing app. Trying to do ML in TS or Auth.js-equivalents in Python would have meant fighting the ecosystem at every step. The cost is two dependency managers, mitigated by uv + npm both being fast.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_2_a: We already had Postgres for everything else, so pgvector was tempting. Qdrant won on filtering ergonomics (named payload fields, hybrid search), and on the fact that it's the dominant choice in production RAG stacks today — easier to find debugging help and tooling.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_3_q: Why was IndoBERT retired and all sentiment moved to Gemini Flash-Lite?
HowItWorks.tradeoff_3_a: It used to be a hybrid: Gemini event-valence for mainstream news, IndoBERT for social posts (X/IG/TikTok/YouTube). But a manual eval on 37 'korupsi' tweets on 2026-05-25 showed IndoBERT mislabelled 6 of 7 positives (sarcasm read as positive, supportive opinion as negative, rhetorical critique as neutral) — positive-class accuracy ~14%. After switching every platform to Gemini Flash-Lite, positive-class accuracy on X jumped to ~92% on the same sample. Extra cost: ~$1/mo for X sentiment, immaterial against the budget. Trade-off paid: torch + transformers + the HuggingFace cache (~1.5 GB worker image) leave the dependency tree, plus the worker cold-start gets ~10-15s faster.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_4_q: Why Gemini Pro primary and Claude Sonnet fallback, not the reverse?
HowItWorks.tradeoff_4_a: Gemini 2.5 Pro is ~35% cheaper than Sonnet 4.5 at comparable quality bracket for this kind of long-form structured-output task. Putting it first lowers the running cost meaningfully; Sonnet's the backstop when Google has an outage.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_5_q: Why not use a framework like LangChain or LlamaIndex?
HowItWorks.tradeoff_5_a: Our retrieval + synthesis flow is ~80 lines of TypeScript. Adding a framework would have added 200 transitive dependencies for value we don't use. We're not chaining many models or supporting many vector DBs — when those needs appear, the trade flips.
HowItWorks.tradeoff_6_q: Why no Facebook scraper on the schedule?
HowItWorks.tradeoff_6_a: A `scrape_facebook()` function exists in `apify.py`, but no beat entry calls it. Three reasons. (1) Facebook has been declining as a content surface for younger Indonesian Muslims — most of the genuinely-active da'wah conversation has migrated to TikTok and X. (2) Apify's Facebook actor is among the priciest per item, and the marginal yield per IDR didn't justify it. (3) Instagram already covers the Meta ecosystem on our active schedule. We keep the scraper code ready, so the day data tells us we're missing something it can be enabled with a one-line beat entry.
HowItWorks.repo_title: Read the source. Send a PR.
HowItWorks.repo_body: The whole repo is on GitHub under the MIT license. Issues, PRs, du'a, and feedback are all welcome — there is no contributor-license-agreement step and no formal review board, just a small team that will read what you send.

Contact.hero_title: Tell us what's on your mind.
Contact.hero_subtitle: Feedback, du'a, a bug, a partnership idea — we read everything. We're a small non-profit team, so allow up to 7 working days for a reply.
Contact.field_message_placeholder: Tell us about your feedback, question, or idea…
Contact.sent_title: Got it — jazakallahu khayran for reaching out.

Contact.footer_note: Your email is used only to reply to you. We do not add you to any mailing list, and we do not log your IP address. See the privacy page for the full rundown.
Contact.beta_access_body: Tell us in the message what you want to do with personalized briefings — which community you serve, your primary audience, and the format that would help most. Each request gets a manual review; beta quota is limited and we reply by email, usually within 1-3 business days.
